passage: A survey of 850 college students examined the relationship between sleep duration and GPA. Students sleeping fewer than 6 hours per night had an average GPA of 2.84; those sleeping 6–7 hours averaged 3.08; and those sleeping 8 or more hours averaged 3.24. The researchers also found that sleep-deprived students were 60% more likely to report missing class and 45% more likely to report difficulty concentrating during lectures.
question: Which finding most directly supports the idea that sleep deprivation affects academic performance through behavioral as well as cognitive pathways?

choices.A: Sleep-deprived students were 60% more likely to miss class (behavioral) and 45% more likely to report difficulty concentrating (cognitive).
choices.B: Students sleeping fewer than 6 hours had an average GPA of 2.84.
choices.C: Students sleeping 8 or more hours averaged the highest GPA at 3.24.

explanation: A directly identifies both a behavioral pathway (missing class) and a cognitive pathway (difficulty concentrating), supporting the claim that sleep deprivation operates through two distinct mechanisms. B and C show the GPA outcome but do not distinguish behavioral from cognitive pathways. D describes sample size—methodology.
